evals.prompt: I just finished developing a new payment app called stripe-payments for the payment domain. The extracted directory is at payment/stripe-payments/commerce-stripe-payments-app-v1.0.0/ and it has an icon at icons/stripe.png. Can you package this app for me? Version is 1.0.0, publisher is Stripe Inc., and the description is 'Accept payments with Stripe'.
evals.expected_output: Creates ZIP at payment/stripe-payments/stripe-payments-v1.0.0.zip with correct folder structure, updates commerce-apps-manifest/manifest.json with iconName matching stripe.png, computes SHA256 hash, adds translations, and validates folder structure matches {domain}/{appName}/ pattern

evals.prompt: The avalara-tax app in tax/avalara-tax/ directory needs to be repackaged. The extracted directory is commerce-avalara-tax-app-v1.0.1/ and I just updated the icon file to avalara-logo.png. Package it up and make sure the root manifest iconName matches the new icon filename.
evals.expected_output: Creates ZIP, updates root manifest with iconName set to avalara-logo.png (matching the actual icon filename), validates icon name consistency, and confirms folder structure is tax/avalara-tax/

evals.prompt: I have a loyalty app that I need to package. It's in the wrong location at loyalty/acme-inc/acme-loyalty-program/. The app name in manifest.json is 'acme-loyalty'. Can you detect this folder structure issue and let me know what's wrong before packaging?
evals.expected_output: Identifies folder structure violation - app should be at loyalty/acme-loyalty/ not loyalty/acme-inc/acme-loyalty-program/, explains the installation URL pattern requirement {domain}/{appName}/, and suggests moving to correct location
